Data model note — Root Cause, Corrective Action and Preventive Action are only captured in the Incidents table, which has no key back to individual Quality Check cases. The charts below combine both sources: error taxonomy and severity come from Quality Checks; root-cause / corrective / preventive views come from Incidents.

Data model note — the Incident table has no Department/Employee key, no Severity attribute, and no "Reported/Opened Date" — only Closed Date. Critical Incidents and Average Closure Time can't be computed without those fields; see the Build Guide for recommended schema additions.

Incident Status

Open vs Closed

Incident Category

By Error Category

Root Cause Distribution

Corrective Action Distribution

Preventive Action Distribution

Closed Incidents by Month

Based on Closed Date — open incidents have no date to bucket by
Methodology: Overall Quality Score = average of Quality Score across cases · SOP/Documentation/SLA/First-Pass/Rework % = share of cases flagged Yes/No on the respective field · Error Rate = share of cases with a logged Error Type · Audit Pass Rate = share of audits scoring ≥ 90 (the source Audit Status field is always "Completed", so pass/fail is inferred from score) · Repeat Errors = error cases beyond the first for a given employee + error-type pair.
Known data-model gaps (see Build Guide for recommended fixes): Quality_Checks has no native date field — a "Check Date" was synthesized (uniform random, seeded, across CY2025) solely to enable monthly trending in this prototype; Incidents has no Department/Employee key, no Severity field, and no reported/opened date.
